issues.body: _retry_after_seconds accepts values such as "NaN" because float parsing succeeds and the negative-value check is false. min(NaN, 60.0) remains NaN, so _sleep_transient calls time.sleep(NaN), which raises ValueError rather than falling back to jitter or ultimately raising GroomError. Retry-After is external response data, so finite-value validation would make the bounded retry path robust to malformed headers.
issues.fix_hint: Reject non-finite parsed values with math.isfinite(seconds) before the range checks, return None for them, and add NaN/Infinity cases to the Retry-After self-test.
issues.title: Non-finite Retry-After values escape the controlled failure path

verdict.summary: The round-1 major finding is resolved. Transparent retries are limited to GET and HEAD, while ambiguous connection and 503 failures on POST/PATCH fail after one attempt. The added mutation assertions are non-vacuous because widening the retryable method set or bypassing the guard changes the observed call count and fails the self-test. Retry exhaustion, non-transient failures, CAS conflicts, and 404 handling remain fail-closed. One minor malformed Retry-After edge remains: Python accepts NaN as a float, after which time.sleep raises ValueError instead of using backoff or the normal GroomError path. This does not weaken a trust decision or justify blocking the otherwise sound fix.
